channel.title: Bloomberg Technology
channel.copyright: Copyright 2025 BLOOMBERG L.P. ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

item.title: Australia Unveils Cyber Security Push for Indo-Pacific Region
item.description: Australia has earmarked A$83.5 million ($54.4 million) to build cyber-security capacity across the Indo-Pacific, saying global cooperation is key as online crimes become more complex and sophisticated.
item.dc:creator: Richard Henderson

media:content.media:description: Cybersecurity. Photographer: Chris Ratcliffe/Bloomberg

item.title: Trump’s Attack on Ontario’s Reagan Ad Helped Amplify Its Reach
item.description: In the global portfolio of political ads that changed history, a handful stand out.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Bochove

media:content.media:description: US President Donald Trump speaks to members of the media on the South Lawn of the White House before boarding Marine One in Washington, DC, US, on Friday, Oct. 24, 2025. Trump will look to sign economic agreements and critical minerals deals with trading partners during his trip to Asia, according to senior US officials, as the administration moves to ramp up pressure on China ahead of a crucial meeting with Xi Jinping.

item.title: ‘This Is Not My First Bubble’: Nvidia’s Bear Stands His Ground
item.description: Many on Wall Street like to style themselves as contrarians, but few are swimming against the current as hard as Jay Goldberg, the lone bear on high-flying Nvidia Corp.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Vlastelica, Jeran Wittenstein

media:content.media:description: Jay Goldberg Photographer: Laure Andrillon/Bloomberg

item.title: Amazon’s AWS Shows Signs of Weakness as Competitors Charge Ahead
item.description: The company that invented the cloud business is widely perceived as trailing its rivals in artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Matt Day

media:content.media:description: An Amazon Web Services data center in Stone Ridge, Virginia, US, on Sunday, July 28, 2024. Data center developers in Northern Virginia are asking utility Dominion Energy Inc. for as much power as several nuclear reactors can generate, in the latest sign of how artificial intelligence is helping drive up electricity demand.

item.title: Fast-Money Quants Stumble as Momentum Bust Roils Strategies
item.description: Behind the scenes of placid Wall Street markets, drama is unfolding for some heavy-hitting professional investors.
item.dc:creator: Justina Lee, Denitsa Tsekova

item.title: Intel Earnings Signal Possible Comeback | Bloomberg Tech 10/24/2025
item.description: Bloomberg’s Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow discuss Intel’s earnings, as the chipmaker returned to profitability and gave an upbeat revenue forecast. Plus, banks are preparing to launch a $38 billion debt offering to help fund data centers tied to Oracle. That comes as the Bank of England begins to probe data center lending amid fears of an AI bubble. And, Mistral CEO Arthur Mensch discusses the company's release of a new platform to help enterprise clients make customizable AI tools. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: $10,000 Card Shufflers Hit the Spotlight After NBA Poker Scandal
item.description: The rigged-poker-game indictment unveiled on Thursday reads like a Hollywood heist film, featuring NBA stars and famous Mafia crime families. It also involves the high-tech hacking of casino-quality card shuffling machines.
item.dc:creator: Molly Schuetz

media:content.media:description: A DeckMate 2 shuffler taken apart on a table, from the federal indictment.

item.title: Intel Rally Fades After Investors Seek More Evidence of Comeback
item.description: An Intel Corp. stock rally faded on Friday after investors looked past an upbeat forecast and focused on the many lingering challenges at the embattled chipmaker.
item.dc:creator: Ian King

media:content.media:description: Intel Corp. headquarters in Santa Clara, California. Photographer: David Paul Morris/Bloomberg

item.title: Nexperia’s Chinese Owner Warns of Fallout From Dutch Seizure
item.description: China’s Wingtech Technology Co. warned the Dutch government’s seizure of chipmaking unit Nexperia may hit its bottomline if it cannot take back control by end of the year.
item.dc:creator: Foster Wong

media:content.media:description: Banner flags with the Nexperia Holding BV logo outside their headquarters in the 52Nijmegen high-rise building on the Noviotech Campus in Nijmegen, Netherlands, on Friday, Oct. 24, 2025. Nexperia notified its Japanese automotive customers that it may no longer be able to guarantee supply, triggering renewed concern about upheaval at the Dutch chipmaker snarling global supply chains.

item.title: Measles Outbreaks Across the US Spread As Vaccination Rates Fall
item.description: Measles outbreaks have spread across the US to reach 41 states. The largest ongoing cluster in Utah and Arizona has now grown into the second-biggest outbreak of the year.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Nix

media:content.media:description: SALT LAKE CITY, UT - APRIL 26: In this photo illustration sterile water is prepared for a one dose bottle of measles, mumps and rubella virus vaccine, made by MERCK, at the Salt Lake County Health Department on April 26, 2019 in Salt Lake City, Utah. (Photo Illustration by George Frey/Getty Images)

item.title: Power Stocks Stumble as Investors See Cracks in AI-Fueled Rally
item.description: For most of the year, it looked like power demand from AI was insatiable, sending investors rushing into shares of the companies that generate electricity. Whether coal-fired, wind-driven or nuclear, generators across the board saw dizzying rallies.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Griffin

media:content.media:description: The sun sets behind utility lines during high temperatures in Columbia, South Carolina, US, on Monday, July 28, 2025. Nearly half the US will wilt under hot, sticky conditions through the bulk of the week as temperatures and humidity soar from Chicago to New York City and New Orleans, boosting power demand and raising health risks.

item.title: Market for ‘Counter-Strike’ Items Falls $3 Billion
item.description: The value of digital items in the popular video-game Counter—Strike has fallen 48% following an update by the title’s developer Valve Corp.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia D'Anastasio

media:content.media:description: A person plays "Counter-Strike: Global Offensive" in Hanover, Germany. Photographer: Michael Matthey/picture alliance/Getty Images

item.title: Carmakers Fight to Keep Going as Nexperia Chip Conflict Drags On
item.description: Europe’s auto industry is working around the clock to prevent the conflict with China over chipmaker Nexperia from triggering production outages.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Behrmann

media:content.media:description: Banner flags outside the Nexperia headquarters in Nijmegen, Netherlands.

item.title: S&P 500 Adds Dicey Stocks as Market Turns Increasingly Exuberant
item.description: Over the past few years, few stocks in the US have been hotter than AppLovin Corp.
item.dc:creator: Carmen Reinicke

media:content.media:description: S&P 500 signage displayed at the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) in New York. Photographer: Michael Nagle/Bloomberg

item.title: Why Making 'The Outer Worlds 2' Took Twice as Long as the First Game
item.description: The well-received sequel will be out next week, six years after the original title
item.dc:creator: Jason Schreier

media:content.media:description: Obsidian’s The Outer Worlds 2 was six years in the making Photographer: Microsoft

item.title: JPMorgan Hires BofA’s Kevin Brunner as Global Chair of M&A
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has hired veteran technology dealmaker Kevin Brunner from Bank of America Corp. as a global chair of investment banking and mergers and acquisitions.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Gould, Liana Baker

media:content.media:description: The new JPMorgan Chase & Co. headquarters in New York.

item.title: Canal+ Said to Explore Buying Out Comcast’s Stake in African Streaming Platform
item.description: Canal+ SA is studying the possibility of buying out the remaining stake in African streaming platform Showmax from Comcast Corp., people familiar with the matter said, as the French media firm looks to consolidate its operations in the continent.
item.dc:creator: Vinicy Chan, Loni Prinsloo

media:content.media:description: A billboard advertises of streaming service Showmax, operated by MultiChoice Group Ltd., in Johannesburg, South Africa, on Wednesday, Dec. 18, 2024. MultiChoice Group is preparing to take on US streaming giants as the African TV company works to get its approximately $3 billion deal with Vivendi SE’s Canal+ over the line with regulators.

item.title: Altcoin Market Falls $800 Billion Short as Retail Traders Vanish
item.description: The altcoin market is $800 billion smaller than past cycles would suggest — and a shift in retail sentiment may have killed it.
item.dc:creator: Suvashree Ghosh

media:content.media:description: A screen showing a Bitcoin price graph and other cryptocurrency market movements at a cryptocurrency exchange in Barcelona, Spain, on Wednesday, Aug. 13, 2025. Bitcoin and Ether are both trading within a whisker of all-time highs as investors pile into the two largest cryptocurrencies, with a recent wave of demand tilting in favor of the latter.

item.title: EU Ramps Up Content Moderation Probes Against Meta, TikTok
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. and ByteDance Ltd.’s TikTok have breached European Union rules designed to tackle illegal or harmful online content, according to early findings from the bloc.
item.dc:creator: Gian Volpicelli

media:content.media:description: The Facebook Inc. application Photographer: Johannes Berg/Bloomberg

item.title: VC Firms Lean on AI for More Than Just Returns
item.description: Welcome to Tech In Depth, our daily newsletter about the business of tech from Bloomberg’s journalists around the world. Today, Kate Clark reports on how venture capital firms are using artificial intelligence technology as they flood AI startups with money.
item.dc:creator: Kate Clark

media:content.media:description: Tim Draper, chairman of DFJ Athena, at the Paris Blockchain Week summit in Paris, France, on Wednesday, April 10, 2024. The summit runs until Thursday, April 11.

item.title: OpenAI Backs a New Venture Trying to Thwart AI Bio-Attacks
item.description: OpenAI, Founders Fund and Lux Capital have invested in Valthos, a startup using AI to try to prevent AI-enabled destruction.
item.dc:creator: Lizette Chapman

media:content.media:description: A lab technician uses a pipette dropper during the antigen quantification process of the coronavirus vaccine research at the Valneva SA laboratories in Vienna, Austria, on Thursday, Aug. 6, 2020. The U.K. has signed agreements to buy 90 million doses of vaccines in development by drugmakers including Pfizer Inc., BioNTech SE and Valneva SE, joining countries around the world racing to secure supplies of protection against Covid-19.

item.title: US Moves to Speed Up AI Power Hookup Process
item.description: The Trump administration is pushing regulators to accelerate the process of allowing for data-center connections to power grids, proposing a 60-day limit to reviews. Matthew Bloxham of Bloomberg Intelligence has more.

item.title: Google TPUs Find Sweet Spot of AI Demand, a Decade After Chip’s Debut
item.description: In an AI chip industry that’s almost entirely commanded by Nvidia Corp., a Google chip first developed more than 10 years ago especially for artificial intelligence tasks is finally gaining momentum outside its home company as a way to train and run complex AI models.
item.dc:creator: Dina Bass, Emily Forgash

item.title: VW Supplier Secures Nexperia Chip Substitutes in Standoff
item.description: Valeo SE has found replacements for chips supplied by Nexperia for almost its entire automotive product range, indicating industry progress on efforts to ward off looming output stoppages.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Behrmann, William Wilkes

media:content.media:description: A Valeo SE logo at the Paris Motor Show. Photographer: Nathan Laine/Bloomberg

item.title: Ex-UBS Chairman Weber Warns AI Ushering In New Era of Inequality
item.description: Former UBS Group AG Chairman Axel Weber has warned the coming disruption from artificial intelligence could usher in the rise of a new global elite that profits disproportionately from the adoption of the cutting-edge technology while leaving the rest worse off.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Zhu, Yujing Liu

media:content.media:description: Axel Weber Photographer: Pascal Mora/Bloomberg

item.title: Thailand Revokes Citizenship of Cambodia Tycoon on Scam Activity
item.description: Thailand’s government revoked citizenship of a Cambodian high-ranking businessman after he was accused of being involved with cyber-scam operations and human trafficking networks.
item.dc:creator: Suttinee Yuvejwattana

media:content.media:description: Anutin Charnvirakul, leader of the Bhumjaithai Party, arrives at parliament in Bangkok, Thailand, on Friday, Sept. 5, 2025. Anutin is poised to win a Thai parliament vote for prime minister on Friday after the conservative party politician cobbled together an unlikely alliance of royalist and pro-democracy groups to break a political deadlock.

item.title: China Aims to Keep ‘Reasonable’ Growth, Boost Consumption Share
item.description: China will seek to maintain a certain rate of growth and expand consumption’s share of the economy as authorities draft the next five-year development blueprint, a senior official said.
item.dc:creator: John Liu, Fran Wang

media:content.media:description: Pedestrians cross a road in Pudong's Lujiazui Financial District in Shangha. Photographer: Qilai Shen/Bloomberg
